lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Here it is: your own garage. One service bay for working on cars, parking for keeping them, a little cash in the till, and the labour bar up top: your working day. Spanner work drains it; a night's sleep (press End Day) refills it.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: The tabs along the top are your world: the office phone where work finds you, the auction rooms, the parts shop. We will visit each one when it matters.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Start on the Phone tab: the shop's office phone. Customers ring with work, and the odd one walks straight in. Yuki is the walk-in kind: a student who has been saving all year and needs four wheels that will pass inspection. She has {payout}.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: She will hand you the whole envelope and trust you with it. Build her something roadworthy for less, and whatever is left over is your pay: every job in this trade works like that.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Accept the job when you are ready. There is no deadline; she is happy to wait.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Now the Auctions tab. There are several auction rooms in town, but a young garage starts in one: the Local Yard. Cheap cars and quick hammers, and exactly the right hunting ground for Yuki's budget.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: There is a {model} on the block that could be just right for her. Nothing glamorous, but read its sheet: it lists an engine tick, and the room can only price that noise at the odds. Prove which tick it is and you know something the price does not; if it is the cheap one, that is a bargain wearing a scary label.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Only one way to find out. Press Inspect here: a small fee and a little labour buys us an hour in the yard with the cars. Grab a coffee from the vending machine on the way in; the truth is worth more than the fee.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Ears first, tools second. Rev it gently and listen: valve gear keeps time, light and regular. Anything deeper, we worry about later. Five minutes.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: A tidy tick, up top. Now the stethoscope on the cam cover: fifteen minutes, and we'll know exactly which bit of the valve gear is talking.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Just lifters: the cheap kind of tick, the kind a quiet afternoon and a set of shims can cure. The room is still scared of it, and their fear is our discount.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Take a seat. It's a quiet morning and nobody else came for her: bid the reserve, the least the seller will take, and let the clock run out. Winning slowly still counts as winning.

lines.speaker: yuki
lines.text: It has a certain something. Under the dirt.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: The {model} is yours, and she is sitting in parking looking sorry for herself. Tools only reach a car in the service bay: drag her across (or open her and press Move to service bay) and let us get a proper look.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Open her from the bay. The big picture of the car is the service diagram: point at the wheels and click, and the panel underneath shows what you can do there.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Start at the ground. Her tyres are scrap, and scrap tyres fail a roadworthy all on their own. Tyres come off with the wheels: press Remove assembly (taking things apart is free, and quietly one of life's pleasures), and the wheels land on the bench below.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Fresh tyres, then. In the Parts tab, open Wheels & tyres and pick Tyres, and set the fits filter to her {model}: the shop will only show what fits her. Press Add to cart on the plain stock tyres (sport rubber is lovely, and entirely not in her budget), then Checkout; standard delivery lands tomorrow morning.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Tyres ordered. Nothing more we can do tonight, and that is fine: press End Day, and they will be on the shelf with the morning.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Morning: your tyres are in. Click the Tyres block on the bench, press Replace, and pick the fresh stock tyres from your parts; the small fee is the fitting shop's machine doing the pressing (one day you will own one, and the fee disappears).

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Fresh rubber on. Press Refit assembly and the wheels go back on, better than she has had in years.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Now for that tick, and this one is proper surgery. It lives in the {part}, deep in the engine, and the engine will not come out with the Intake, the Exhaust and the Cooling bolted on around it. Click each of those three on the diagram and press Take it off; taking parts off costs nothing, and they wait safely in your inventory.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: With those three off, click the engine and press Remove assembly. No crane of your own yet, so the machine shop next door lends its arm for a fee, out and back in.

lines.speaker: yuki
lines.text: My uncle had an engine crane once. He also had a roof. Long story.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Engine out. Click the Head on the bench and press the Repair button; one rung, poor to worn, is all her inspection asks, and the price and labour are written on it. Then press Refit assembly: the shop's arm again for the way back in.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: The repair and the refit are where the labour goes; taking things off never costs any. If the bar runs dry before you are done, no matter: press End Day, and the morning brings a fresh one.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Nearly home; the Intake, the Exhaust and the Cooling are still on your shelf. Click each empty slot on the diagram, press Replace and pick the part you took off, then press Confirm. Putting back exactly what came off costs nothing, and she is not roadworthy until she is whole.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: Back to the Phone tab. Pick the {model} on her card and press Show them the car; she will look it over, and when every line reads green, press Hand it over. Go on: she has waited long enough.

lines.speaker: instruction
lines.text: That is the walkthrough done, and your first happy customer on the road. From tomorrow the phone starts ringing; do good work and the town will find its way to your door.
